metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full (model NAME); driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (for model IDENTIFICATION — the model name)
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

Model identification for mistral-vibe is the model NAME, distinct from the context-WINDOW capacity bar (scenario 1.8 / model-context-display, which needs the capacity map). This cell asserts only that `SessionMetrics.ModelName` reflects the launched model and is not 'unknown'.

**agent = yes.** Vibe persists the active model in the per-session `meta.json` sidecar under `config.active_model`. Confirmed from the LIVE surface (6/6 live sessions carry `"active_model": "mistral-medium-3.5"`). `config.models[]` lists name/alias pairs — `active_model` is the ALIAS; the underlying `name` for the default is `mistral-vibe-cli-latest` (also available: `devstral-small`, `local`). A single no-op turn is enough to elicit the model — the sidecar is written from launch and rewritten after every turn.

**daemon = full (model name).** The vibe parser enriches EVERY emitted event with the model via `applySidecar`: it reads `config.active_model` from the sibling meta.json (`sidecar.go` decodes `Config.ActiveModel`) and sets `ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(st.model)` on every event (`parser.go` L108-111). `NormalizeModelName` only rewrites known Claude aliases/patterns and otherwise returns the string unchanged (parser.go L555-573 default `return normalized`), so `mistral-medium-3.5` passes through intact. So the daemon KNOWS and displays the model id from the first event onward — the canonical model-identification assertion (`ModelName != 'unknown'`) is met.

**driver = gap:wait_turn.** The model-identification recipe is the basic-turn arc: `send` a trivial prompt, `wait_turn`, `sleep`. The freshly-scaffolded interactive driver ports only `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`; `wait_turn` is stubbed (`driver-interactive.sh` L168-169 `not_implemented wait_turn`). The recipe needs `wait_turn`, so the driver is gap:wait_turn. This is tooling work (port SEAM 2), NOT an observability limit — the cell is a real cell with a real recipe; `record` ports the turn-done poll before driving.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (out of scope here)

- **Context-window capacity** — model-context-display (1.8/5.7), not this cell. The sidecar's `stats.context_tokens` gives context USAGE (emitted as `Tokens.Total` on turn_done), but the WINDOW capacity for mistral models is not in `core/pkg/capacity`, so the utilization bar's denominator is unresolved. The model NAME is unaffected.
- **Cost** — the parser never reads `stats.session_cost`; mistral-vibe is absent from `core/pkg/capacity`. Cost is not observable (cost_nonzero=false in the spec) — but the model name still displays.
- Timestamps, Control (backchannel unverified — read-only adapter), subagent-linking.
caveats: This cell asserts the model NAME only. ContextWindow capacity resolution for mistral models is a separate concern (scenario model-context-display / capacity map) and is NOT asserted here.

PROVISIONAL on emission timing: whether a transient proc-<PID> presession row precedes the session_<...> transcript-directory birth is not confirmable from source; modeled single-birth (like claudecode/kiro-cli) — record settles it.
config.active_model is the ALIAS (mistral-medium-3.5); the underlying config.models[].name is mistral-vibe-cli-latest. The daemon surfaces the alias, which is the user-facing model id.

sources.note: applySidecar sets ev.ModelName = tailer.NormalizeModelName(st.model) on EVERY event, keeping the model lit between turns.
readSidecar decodes Config.ActiveModel from meta.json config.active_model.
NormalizeModelName leaves non-Claude ids (mistral-medium-3.5) intact — default return normalized, no mistral rewrite.
LIVE surface: 6/6 live sessions carry active_model=mistral-medium-3.5; config.models[] pairs alias mistral-medium-3.5 with name mistral-vibe-cli-latest (also devstral-small, local).
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn arm is not_implemented (stubbed) — recipe needs wait_turn → driver=gap:wait_turn.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (v2.19.0) and authenticated against Mistral
irrlichd is recording (isolated mode or --attach against a --record daemon)
the mistral-vibe interactive driver has SEAM 2 (wait_turn) ported before recording (currently stubbed)
setup: Driver launches vibe in a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so a fresh session_<...> transcript directory is minted
The active model (config.active_model) is the account default mistral-medium-3.5; no model flag needed to identify the model
ModelName is checked AFTER turn_end (the sidecar is written from launch, so ModelName is lit from the first event)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl shows one ready→working→ready cycle on the session_<...> id
SessionMetrics.ModelName after turn_end equals mistral-medium-3.5 (from meta.json config.active_model via applySidecar), NOT 'unknown'
transcript messages.jsonl contains 1 user line and >= 1 assistant line
